abstract: Saves one or more subscriptions to record changes.
overview:
## Return Value

A `Promise` object that resolves to a [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.SubscriptionsResponse] object if the operation succeeds; otherwise, a [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit/CKError] object.

## Discussion

Instead of fetching records, you can subscribe to changes, and let the server run the query in the background. You subscribe to changes by creating a subscription, represented by a [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Subscription] dictionary, and saving it to a database. Then you register for push notifications and handle them when they arrive.

Subscribing to Zone Changes

To subscribe to all changes in a zone, set the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Subscription] dictionary’s `subscriptionType` key to `zone` and set the `zoneID` key to the name of the zone you want to observe.

Save the subscription using the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Database/saveSubscriptions] method.

To get the saved subscription, use the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.SubscriptionsResponse/subscriptions] property in the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.SubscriptionsResponse] class.

Subscribing to Record Changes Using a Query

Create a query subscription object (a [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Subscription] dictionary with the `subscriptionType` key set to `query`) specifying the record type, matching criteria, and types of changes you want to be notified about. Then save the query subscription object to the database.

For example, subscribe to receive new artwork from an artist where the `artist` field in the `Artwork` record type is a `Reference` type. First create a reference to the `Artist` record to use in the query.

Create a query subscription object specifying `Artwork` records whose `artist` field matches the `Reference` object. Use the `firesOn` key to specify the types of operations that fire this subscription.

Save the subscription using the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Database/saveSubscriptions] method and get the saved subscription using the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.SubscriptionsResponse/subscriptions] property in the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.SubscriptionsResponse] class.

Handling Subscription Push Notifications

Add a notification listener to the app’s container using the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Container/addNotificationListener] method in the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Container] class and implement the listener to handle the push notifications.

For details on the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Notification] object passed to the listener, read [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Notification].

Registering for Subscription Push Notifications

Saving subscriptions to the database doesn’t automatically configure your web app to receive notifications when a subscription fires. CloudKit uses the Apple Push Notification service (APNs) to send notifications, so your web app needs to register for push notifications to receive them.

To receive push notifications when a subscription fires, use the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Container/registerForNotifications] method in the [doc://com.apple.cloudkitjs/documentation/CloudKitJS/CloudKit.Container] class.

items.description: Fetches all subscriptions in the schema.
items.name: fetchAllSubscriptions

title: saveSubscriptions
